title: Alnylam — ATTR — Thesis
html:
ATTR Amyloidosis: Forward-Looking Thesis

Five strategic bets that will shape ZoomRx's consulting value to Alnylam over the next 12-24 months.

Thesis 1: The Silencer-Stabilizer Combination Paradigm Will Become Standard of Care Within 18 Months

The bet: By late 2027, the dominant treatment paradigm for ATTR-CM will shift from monotherapy (either stabilizer OR silencer) to combination therapy (silencer + stabilizer). AMVUTTRA + VYNDAMAX/ATTRUBY will emerge as the de facto standard for moderate-to-severe ATTR-CM patients.

Evidence supporting this:

  • ZoomRx PCA data (Q2 2026) already shows AMVUTTRA + VYNDAMAX (24 CM charts) and AMVUTTRA + ATTRUBY (16 CM charts) in real-world use
  • The mechanisms are complementary: silencers reduce TTR production at source while stabilizers prevent remaining TTR from misfolding. Combined, they address both existing and new TTR protein
  • Alnylam's own strategic imperative 1.2 positions AMVUTTRA as "foundational therapy across treatment paradigm" -- this language implies combination, not replacement
  • Oncology precedent: combination therapy almost always supplants monotherapy in diseases with multiple actionable pathways

What ZoomRx should track:

  • PCA combination chart share over time (currently ~15% of CM charts -- watch for inflection above 30%)
  • ATU questions on physician attitudes toward combination vs. monotherapy
  • Payer coverage decisions on combination reimbursement
  • Whether Alnylam starts formally positioning AMVUTTRA as "add-on" vs. "switch"

Risk if wrong: If combination therapy does not gain traction (e.g., due to payer pushback on dual cost or lack of combination trial data), AMVUTTRA and ATTRUBY/VYNDAMAX remain in direct competition for monotherapy position. This is a higher-stakes zero-sum fight.

Thesis 2: CARDIO-TTRansform Will Be Positive But Insufficient to Displace AMVUTTRA

The bet: The Wainua (eplontersen) ATTR-CM Phase 3 trial will show positive results (expected mid-2026), achieving statistical significance on the primary composite endpoint. However, the effect size will be smaller than HELIOS-B, giving AMVUTTRA a sustained clinical differentiation advantage.

Evidence supporting this:

  • Eplontersen achieves ~80% TTR knockdown at trough vs. AMVUTTRA's ~88% sustained. This 8-point gap may translate into a clinically meaningful difference in outcome magnitude
  • HELIOS-B set a high bar: 28% reduction in death + CV events overall, 33% in monotherapy. CARDIO-TTRansform would need to match or exceed this to displace the first-mover advantage
  • Self-administration (monthly auto-injector) is eplontersen's key differentiator, not superior efficacy. This positions it as a "convenience alternative" rather than a "better therapy"
  • Alnylam has >12,000 patients on therapy and $4.4-4.7B 2026 revenue guidance. Wainua would need a dramatically superior dataset to cause meaningful switching

What ZoomRx should track:

  • CARDIO-TTRansform readout timing and headline data (expected mid-2026)
  • If positive: track physician switching intent, head-to-head perception surveys, Wainua PET activation
  • If negative/underwhelming: track how quickly Alnylam messaging pivots to highlight AMVUTTRA's proven superiority
  • Monitor the US PET for Wainua -- fielding was paused at 103% completion in Q2 2026; will resume post-data

Risk if wrong: If CARDIO-TTRansform shows unexpected superiority (e.g., better mortality signal or dramatic subgroup findings), the self-administered advantage becomes a killer combination with better efficacy. This would be a significant headwind for AMVUTTRA.

Thesis 3: The Cardiologist Conversion Is Alnylam's Make-or-Break Challenge

The bet: AMVUTTRA's trajectory will be determined less by clinical data and more by Alnylam's ability to win cardiologists. The neurology-to-cardiology transition is the defining commercial challenge for the next two years, and the international PET data shows Alnylam is still in the early innings of this conversion.

Evidence supporting this:

  • ATTR-CM is a 200,000-patient opportunity in the US alone (vs. ~50,000 for hATTR-PN globally). Cardiologists control the majority of ATTR-CM prescribing
  • Across all five international PET markets, AMVUTTRA's SOV among cardiologists trails its SOV among neurologists by 12-34 percentage points
  • Pfizer's cardiology sales infrastructure vastly outscales Alnylam's in every market
  • ATTRUBY (BridgeBio) enters with cardiology-native positioning -- BridgeBio built its entire commercial operation around cardiology from day one
  • However, the "perception paradox" offers hope: cardiologists who DO see AMVUTTRA details are highly receptive (91% improved perception in Japan, 71% in Spain). The problem is reach, not receptivity

What ZoomRx should track:

  • Cardiologist-specific SOV trending in every market (the most important metric)
  • AMVUTTRA message recall/effectiveness among cardiologists vs. neurologists
  • Alnylam's cardiology field force expansion timing
  • Whether ATU data shows AMVUTTRA awareness among cardiologists closing the gap with neurologists

Risk if wrong: If Alnylam cannot meaningfully close the cardiologist reach gap, ATTRUBY and VYNDAMAX will own the cardiology audience, and AMVUTTRA's growth will plateau at the neurology ceiling. The TTR franchise revenue guidance would be at risk.

Thesis 4: Tafamidis's Decline Will Accelerate, Creating a Share Grab Window

The bet: Pfizer's tafamidis franchise (VYNDAQEL/VYNDAMAX) will decline faster than consensus expects, driven by: (a) VYNDAQEL US discontinuation in 2025, (b) ATTRUBY's "better stabilizer" narrative eroding new patient starts, (c) AMVUTTRA's mortality data in ATTR-CM, and (d) approaching 2028 patent cliff. This creates a 12-18 month window where share is actively in play.

Evidence supporting this:

  • Pfizer discontinued VYNDAQEL in the US (end 2025). While VYNDAMAX continues, the brand simplification signals preparation for patent cliff defense
  • US PET data (Q4 2024) shows Pfizer reach declining -7 pts quarter-over-quarter -- the steepest decline of any manufacturer
  • ATTRUBY achieved $362M in its first full year and 7,800+ prescriptions by Feb 2026. It is specifically positioned as the "better stabilizer" for newly diagnosed patients
  • International PET data from Spain shows AMVUTTRA already flipped SOV from 35% to 56% in one year. This can be replicated in other markets during the tafamidis decline window
  • AMVUTTRA's ATTR-CM approval (March 2025 US, June 2025 EU) gives it the label to compete directly for tafamidis's patient base for the first time

What ZoomRx should track:

  • Tafamidis new patient starts vs. AMVUTTRA and ATTRUBY new patient starts (PCA source-of-share)
  • ATU data on stabilizer-only users' awareness of and attitudes toward silencer therapy
  • International SOV trends for VYNDAQEL -- does it decline uniformly or are there market-specific dynamics?
  • Payer behavior: Are formularies starting to prefer AMVUTTRA or ATTRUBY over tafamidis?

Risk if wrong: Tafamidis has enormous incumbent inertia. Physicians comfortable with an oral daily pill may not switch to quarterly injections regardless of clinical data. If tafamidis holds better than expected, the ATTR-CM market becomes a three-way steady-state rather than a dynamic share grab.

Thesis 5: International Markets Will Stratify Into "RNAi-First" and "Stabilizer-First" Geographies

The bet: Rather than a uniform global competitive landscape, ATTR treatment patterns will diverge geographically: some markets will adopt RNAi (AMVUTTRA) as preferred first-line therapy, while others will maintain stabilizers as the default. This stratification will persist for at least 3-5 years and should shape Alnylam's country-level commercial strategy.

Predicted stratification:

RNAi-First Markets Stabilizer-First Markets Battleground Markets
France (neurology heritage, ONPATTRO loyalty, RNAi advocacy) Japan (oral preference, tafamidis heritage, low patient loads) Germany (parity positioning, price-sensitive)
Spain (academic-driven, rapid AMVUTTRA uptake, SOV lead) Italy (Pfizer infrastructure advantage, slower adoption) US (four-way competition, combination therapy emerging)
Portugal, Sweden, Brazil (endemic hATTR populations, RNAi natural fit) UK (NICE cost-effectiveness frameworks)

Evidence supporting this:

  • ZoomRx PET data shows >20-point SOV differences between Spain (AMVUTTRA 56%) and Japan (AMVUTTRA 41%) -- these reflect structural preferences, not temporary gaps
  • French neurologists achieve 100% Alnylam reach and prefer ONPATTRO's IV profile alongside AMVUTTRA -- this is a market structurally oriented toward gene silencing
  • Japan's physician population treats very low patient volumes (median 2 patients) and favors oral medications -- structural barriers to injectable uptake
  • Reimbursement systems differ: France's early access programs historically favor novel mechanisms; Japan's NHI pricing favors established products

What ZoomRx should track:

  • Country-level prescribing share trends (ATU/PCA equivalent in international markets)
  • Whether the stratification holds or converges as ATTR-CM indications expand internationally
  • Country-specific physician stated preference surveys: MOA preference (silencer vs. stabilizer)
  • Impact of ATTRUBY/BEYONTTRA international launches (2026-2027) -- does it pull stabilizer-first markets further away from RNAi or create new competitive dynamics?

Risk if wrong: If AMVUTTRA's ATTR-CM mortality data proves universally compelling (the "HELIOS-B everywhere" scenario), geographic stratification may not hold. All markets could converge toward RNAi-first. This would be the best outcome for Alnylam but would reduce the value of country-specific consulting.

Thesis Monitoring Dashboard

Thesis Key Signal to Watch Next Data Point Current Confidence
1. Combination SOC PCA combo chart % >30% Q2 2026 PCA delivery (June) Medium-High
2. CARDIO-TTRansform positive but not superior Headline data release Mid-2026 Medium
3. Cardiologist conversion is make-or-break Cardiologist SOV in all markets Q2 2026 PET delivery (June-July) High
4. Tafamidis accelerated decline SOV decline >5 pts/quarter Q2 2026 PET + US ATU Medium
5. Geographic stratification persists SOV variance >15 pts across markets Q4 2026 international PET Medium-High
